mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following easing periods are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its 3-month trailing minimum (a Sahm-rule style trigger), it signals an emerging labor market downturn that leads investors to price in recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp above its trailing 3-month minimum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the average post-trigger return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer and housing demand weakness that individually may be noisy but jointly signals a coordinated demand contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. Neither series alone is sufficient; the interaction of simultaneous declines is required.
failure_condition: Joint-decline episodes produce 20-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional distribution (p > 0.05), or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, sustained INDPRO growth above trend signals capacity utilization pressures and looming supply-side inflation risk, prompting markets to discount future earnings more heavily and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. In early-cycle recoveries the same INDPRO strength instead signals healthy growth and positive returns, making the mechanism regime-dependent.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth > 3% while in late-cycle regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following strong INDPRO growth in late-cycle regime are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the sign of the effect fails to flip when tested separately in early-cycle regime windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains elevated while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation is still above target, the implied real policy rate is restrictive; this joint condition signals continued monetary tightening pressure on valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week. Neither FEDFUNDS level nor CPI level alone captures the restrictive stance; their combination does.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during triggered restrictive-real-rate periods are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or interaction model underperforms single-series FEDFUNDS-only model on out-of-sample Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market expansion supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week regardless of broader macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation reverses in more than one 3-year subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below a critical threshold signals a contraction in housing-driven demand and forward-looking construction activity, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following HOUST threshold breaches are not significantly negative versus unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or fewer than 55% of triggered instances show negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between INDPRO deceleration and continued PAYEMS growth signals a productivity slowdown where labor input is rising faster than output, indicating margin compression risk for corporations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. This requires the joint pattern of falling industrial production alongside still-expanding employment; neither series alone captures the margin-compression signal.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with this divergence pattern are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the interaction signal shows no incremental predictive power over INDPRO-only or PAYEMS-only baselines.
